truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 9 vulnerabilities from the 20 true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-001 through SC-V3-020). All 9 findings are supported by source evidence, but 11 true vulnerabilities were missed entirely. Multiple severity underclaims exist (4 findings rated lower than truth). No false positive findings were present — all cited code exists and the vulnerability descriptions are accurate, though severity assessments were incorrect on several items.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 237-238: `std::string active_format = runtime_log_format(); printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);` - The format string is loaded from config (line 209: `static std::string active_format = config::security::LOG_FORMAT;`) and can be modified via set_runtime_log_format() which is called from config file loading (line 556). An attacker controlling the config file can inject format specifiers like %n or %x.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format-string injection via runtime_log_format() and printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input). The quote matches the source lines 237-238 exactly. Finding is complete.
The integer overflow in factorial computation (result *= i with long long overflow) at lines 315-319 was not identified in Run 2. No finding mentions factorial, result *= i, or CWE-190.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 881-888: `void cleanup() { stage_deferred_history_flush(); memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup(); ... }` - stage_deferred_history_flush() (lines 813-828) enqueues tasks that capture `scratch` pointer allocated from g_memory_pool. Immediately after, memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() (line 884) frees all blocks. Worker threads may still access freed memory.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the use-after-free where stage_deferred_history_flush() enqueues tasks capturing scratch pointer, then memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() frees all blocks. The quote matches source lines 881-888 and 813-828.

The command injection via validate_config() (validator_cmd + ' ' + config_file; system(command.c_str()) at lines 563-569) was not identified. Run 2 found admin exec injection but not the config validator injection path.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 831-835: `if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }` - When expression length >= 1024 (STACK_BUFFER_SIZE), strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the 1024-byte stack buffer.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow via strcpy when expression length >= sizeof(input_buffer_). The quote matches source lines 831-835 exactly.

The race condition in ThreadSafeCounter (volatile long long, old_value = value_, lock_guard, value_ = old_value + 1 at lines 478-487) was not identified. Run 2 did not mention volatile, ThreadSafeCounter, or this race pattern.
The logic bomb (login_attempts_ > 5; EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE; authenticated_ = true at lines 592-598) was not identified in Run 2.
Path traversal via config_dir_ + filename in load_config() (lines 542-543) was not identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 367: `expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_));` with buffer_size_ = 256 (line 366). Line 376: `if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());` - Concatenated token string can exceed 256 bytes, causing heap buffer overflow.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in ExpressionParser via strcpy with 256-byte buffer. The quote matches source lines 366-376.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 73: `constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";` - Admin password is hardcoded in source code and visible in binary.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded admin credentials. The quote matches source line 73 exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 635-640: `std::string generate_session_token() { std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); return "TOKEN_" + std::to_string(dis(gen)); }` - Session token is only 4 random digits, easily brute-forceable (10,000 possibilities).
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the weak PRNG for session tokens. The quote matches source lines 635-640 exactly.
Thread-unsafe static std::tm cached_time with localtime() at lines 230-231 was not identified.
Insecure temporary file handling via TEMP/TMPDIR env vars and supercalc_emergency.log in append_override_audit_event() (lines 622-633) was not identified.
Persistent authentication state (authenticated_, login_attempts_, session_token_) was not identified as a standalone finding.
Unhandled exception in std::stod(tokens[3]) in handle_variable_command() (line 729) was not identified.
Integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate() block split (previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES at lines 128-136) was not identified.
Unbounded recursion in parser (parse_expression -> parse_factor -> PAREN_OPEN -> parse_expression at lines 381-433) was not identified.
Data race in result_cache_ accessed without cache_mutex_ in evaluate_cached() (lines 445-452) while thread_pool_->enqueue() runs concurrently was not identified.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 544-547: `if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25)); std::ifstream file(full_path);` - Check-then-use pattern with deliberate delay creates window for symlink attacks or file replacement between existence check and file open.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the TOCTOU race condition. The quote matches source lines 544-547 exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 749-753: `int calculate_route_score(const std::string& input) const { size_t token_end = input.find_first_of(" \t"); size_t token_length = (token_end == std::string::npos) ? input.length() : token_end; return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length); }` - If input is empty, token_length is 0, causing division by zero.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the integer division by zero. The quote matches source lines 749-753 exactly.
